title: Rust on Linen
timeEstimate: 20–45 minutes; oxalic acid bounded; NO chlorine bleach (sets rust); structured = pro
stainChemistry: Rust on linen is iron oxide bonded to the cellulose fiber — usually from a metal hanger, leaky pipe, rusty zipper, or wet metal contact. The recurring traps: chlorine bleach (LOCKS rust into a permanent darker stain — chlorine + iron is the classic irreversible failure), 3% peroxide (oxidizes iron further, makes rust WORSE), and hot water (sets iron-fiber bond). The right tool is REDUCING chemistry — oxalic acid (Bar Keepers Friend, Iron Out, Whink Rust Stain Remover). Linen-specific: structured / lined / dry-clean-only / vintage linen narrows the bench (oxalic can stress the fiber under prolonged contact) — pro from minute one. AIR-DRY FLAT is non-negotiable on linen.
whyThisWorks: Oxalic acid pulls iron oxide off cellulose fibers — it's the OPPOSITE of bleach (bleach oxidizes iron, locking rust; oxalic acid reduces iron, releasing it). Bounded application on simple flat washable white linen + cool rinse + AIR-DRY FLAT is the home protocol. Cool wash AFTER chemistry is safe on white linen. Honest cap: aged set rust with fiber-deep penetration sometimes plateaus.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): rust is Fe³⁺ iron oxide bonded to cellulose. Pro uses oxalic acid (BKF, Iron Out, Whink) at controlled dilution + cool rinse + acetic acid neutralization. Sodium hydrosulfite is the alternative reducing bleach for delicate fibers.
defaultAssumption: Rust spot from a metal hanger / leaky pipe / rusty zipper on a simple flat washable white linen napkin / shirt / tablecloth. Structured / lined / dry-clean-only / vintage linen = pro.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Dry-clean-only / structured / lined / vintage linen = pro. Simple flat washable linen = home candidate. Gloves on — oxalic acid is a skin irritant.

spottingProtocol.equipment: BKF or Iron Out per label on Q-tip
spottingProtocol.instruction: Inside seam: dab oxalic acid solution on white cloth, press 30 sec. Color or fiber damage = skip oxalic, pro for residual rust. White linen usually passes; dyed linen often doesn't.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Bar Keepers Friend / Iron Out / Whink per label, gloves
spottingProtocol.instruction: Mix oxalic acid product per label. Apply to spot — never saturate. 5–10 min. Cool water rinse THOROUGHLY (oxalic acid skin irritant).

spottingProtocol.equipment: Washing machine, cool water, drying rack
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cool machine wash. AIR-DRY FLAT — linen wrinkles aggressively when hung wet. Inspect dry. NEVER hot dryer.

steps: 1. Care label triage — structured / vintage = pro.
2. Gloves + hidden-seam oxalic acid test on dyed linen.
3. Oxalic acid pretreat (BKF / Iron Out / Whink) per label, 5–10 min.
4. Cool water rinse THOROUGHLY (oxalic acid skin irritant).
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — heat sets iron-fiber bond
warnings: Structured / lined / vintage linen = pro from minute one
NEVER chlorine bleach on rust — LOCKS rust permanently
NEVER 3% peroxide on rust — oxidizes iron further, makes worse
NEVER hot water — sets iron-fiber bond
Oxalic acid is a skin irritant — gloves required, rinse thoroughly
NEVER mix oxalic acid with chlorine bleach — toxic chlorine gas
Hidden-seam test on dyed linen before oxalic

homeSolutions.agent: Reducing Chemistry, Not Bleach — Oxalic Acid
homeSolutions.instruction: Rust on linen is iron oxide bonded to the fiber. The most common home failure is chlorine bleach — chlorine + iron LOCKS rust into a permanent darker stain (irreversible). Peroxide is the second-most-common failure — oxidizes iron and makes rust WORSE. The right tool is REDUCING chemistry: oxalic acid (Bar Keepers Friend, Iron Out, or Whink). Two hard rules: NO chlorine bleach (locks rust), NO peroxide (makes rust worse). Structured / lined / dry-clean-only / vintage linen goes to pro from minute one.
Find the care tag. Dry-clean-only / structured / lined / vintage linen = pro. Simple flat washable white linen = home candidate. Gloves on before any oxalic acid handling — it's a skin irritant.
Inside seam: dab oxalic acid (BKF paste or Iron Out) on a white cloth, press 30 seconds. Color or fiber damage transfers = skip oxalic, pro from there. White linen usually passes.

homeSolutions.agent: Bar Keepers Friend or Iron Out — Bounded
homeSolutions.instruction: Bar Keepers Friend (powder, mix to paste with water) OR Iron Out (liquid) OR Whink Rust Stain Remover. Apply to the spot — never saturate the linen. Press 5 to 10 minutes. Cool water rinse THOROUGHLY — oxalic acid is a skin irritant and the residue needs to come out.
Cool machine wash with normal detergent. AIR-DRY FLAT — linen wrinkles aggressively when hung wet, hanging stretches the fiber. Inspect dry. NEVER hot dryer until cleared.

steps: 1. Structured / vintage linen = pro from minute one. NO chlorine bleach. NO peroxide.
2. Gloves on (oxalic acid is skin irritant).
4. Bar Keepers Friend / Iron Out / Whink per label, 5–10 min.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop on dry-clean-only / structured / lined / vintage linen (pro). Stop if hidden-seam test shows damage. Stop if chlorine bleach or peroxide was already used (locked / made worse — irreversible). Stop after 2 home cycles plateau.
warnings: Structured / vintage linen = pro from minute one
NEVER chlorine bleach on rust — LOCKS rust permanently
NEVER 3% peroxide on rust — makes rust WORSE
NEVER hot water — sets iron-fiber bond
Oxalic acid is skin irritant — gloves + thorough rinse
NEVER mix oxalic acid with chlorine — toxic chlorine gas

neverDo: Never chlorine bleach on rust — LOCKS rust permanently
Never 3% peroxide on rust — oxidizes iron further
Never mix oxalic acid with chlorine — toxic chlorine gas
fiberSensitivities: White / light washable linen: tolerates oxalic acid bounded
Structured / lined linen: pro — wet handling warps
Linen-rayon blend: pro — rayon doesn't tolerate oxalic

materialWarnings: Bleach LOCKS rust permanently — most common irreversible failure
Peroxide makes rust WORSE — second-most-common failure
Oxalic acid (BKF / Iron Out / Whink) is the right reducing tool
Structured / vintage linen = pro from minute one

escalation.when: Dry-clean-only / structured / lined / vintage linen. Chlorine bleach already used (locked rust). Peroxide already used (made worse). Hidden-seam test fails. 2 home cycles plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Rust on [white / dyed / structured / vintage] linen, source [hanger / pipe / zipper / metal contact], age [fresh / weeks / months], home-treated with [BKF / Iron Out per label + cool rinse + air-dry flat]. NO chlorine bleach, NO peroxide. [Residual rust / locked by chlorine / vintage piece]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with iron-removal capability

contextQuestions: Simple flat washable linen, structured / lined, or vintage?
Source of rust — hanger, pipe, zipper, metal contact?
Has chlorine bleach or peroxide been applied (often irreversible)?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Rust is the rare REDUCTION chemistry stain — bleach and peroxide both make it worse. Oxalic acid is the right tool. Linen-rayon blends go to pro because rayon doesn't tolerate oxalic. AIR-DRY FLAT is non-negotiable on linen — hanging stretches the fiber.
